intro: Sunday afternoon. The apartment is quiet. You've got nothing scheduled.

scenarios.title: The Starting Point
scenarios.subtitle: How it begins.
scenarios.prompt: You've been thinking about a new project for a while. You sit down to start it.

choices.text: Dive in. I'll figure it out as I go.
Open a blank doc and start planning. Research first, create later.
Stare at the screen. The idea is there but I can't get it out.
Browse other people's work first. I need something to spark it.

scenarios.title: The Messy Middle
scenarios.subtitle: The part nobody posts about.
scenarios.prompt: You're an hour in. The initial excitement is fading and now it's just the work. The messy, slow part.

choices.text: Keep pushing. This is where it actually gets made.
choices.reaction: This is where it actually gets made.

choices.text: Take a break and come back to it later.
choices.reaction: Sometimes the best move is walking away.

choices.text: Start over. This isn't going the way I pictured it.
choices.reaction: Ctrl+A. Delete. Here we go again.

choices.text: Get distracted by a different idea that suddenly feels more exciting.

scenarios.title: The Feedback Problem
scenarios.subtitle: Is it any good?
scenarios.prompt: You've been working on this for a few days. It's starting to take shape. You're not sure if it's good.

choices.text: Send it to someone I trust and ask what they think.
choices.reaction: Waiting for the reply is the hard part.

choices.text: Post it somewhere and see how people react.
Compare it to similar work online. Mine feels worse.
Keep it to myself. It's not ready for anyone else to see.

scenarios.title: The Unfinished Projects
scenarios.subtitle: Be honest.
scenarios.prompt: You get home and open your files. There's the current project, but also three others you never finished.

choices.text: Close them. I'm finishing this one first.
Look through them. Maybe one of these is worth picking back up.
Start thinking about a new idea instead. Here we go again.
Feel the weight of all of it. Nothing ever gets done.
choices.reaction: It's heavier than it should be.

scenarios.title: The Time Problem
scenarios.subtitle: Life gets in the way.
scenarios.prompt: A busy week hits. Work, errands, life. You haven't touched the project in days.

choices.text: Wake up early or stay up late. I make time no matter what.
choices.reaction: You make time. That's the deal.

choices.text: Squeeze in 20 minutes here and there when I can.
Wait until the weekend. I need a real block of time.
The longer I'm away from it the harder it is to go back.

scenarios.title: The Creative Block
scenarios.subtitle: When nothing's working.
scenarios.prompt: The week clears. You sit back down to work on it. Nothing. The ideas aren't coming. You're just staring.

choices.text: Force it. Write something, make something, even if it's bad.
Walk away. It'll come back when it's ready.
Go look at other people's work for inspiration.
Start wondering if I'm even good at this.
choices.reaction: You are. This part just sucks.

scenarios.title: The Finish Line
scenarios.subtitle: Almost there.
scenarios.prompt: You're close to done. All that's left is the unglamorous stuff: editing, cleanup, the details. Not perfect, but close. You can feel the finish line.

choices.text: Lock in and push through the last stretch.
Keep tweaking. It's not ready yet.
choices.reaction: One more pass. Then one more.

choices.text: Step away from it. I need fresh eyes before I call it done.
Lose motivation right at the end. This always happens.

scenarios.title: The Finished Product
scenarios.subtitle: It's done.
scenarios.prompt: You finally finish something. It's done. You're looking at it.

choices.text: Start sharing it. Time to put it out there.
choices.reaction: It's yours. Now it's theirs too.

choices.text: Already thinking about what I'd do differently next time.
choices.reaction: Never fully satisfied. That's the engine.

choices.text: Sit with it for a while. This feels good.
It's done but I'm not sure it's good enough to show anyone.
choices.reaction: It's done. That's enough for now.
